workflows.name: Standard Mission
workflows.tagline: The full guild pipeline for serious, multi-step missions.
workflows.when: Use this when the work is large, multi-disciplinary, or needs several specialists coordinated in waves.

steps.title: Place the Order
steps.act: You describe the mission in plain language so the guild knows what to build.

steps.title: Rewrite as Clear Brief
steps.act: The Butler rewrites your request into an unambiguous, actionable brief with success criteria.

steps.label: You review and approve the brief before the guild begins planning.

steps.title: Form the Campaign Plan
steps.act: The Strategist breaks the mission into waves and assigns each specialist a role that fits their speciality.

steps.title: Distribute the Assignments
steps.act: The Butler routes each slice of the plan to the right specialist with clear, scoped instructions.

steps.label: The Quartermaster certifies the plan is buildable, complete, and safe to start construction.

steps.title: Implement the Plan
steps.act: The Developer writes the code, fixes any bugs, and self-tests everything before handing it back.

steps.title: Clean Up and Verify
steps.act: The Quartermaster runs final cleanup checks and confirms the whole mission works end to end.

steps.label: The Butler delivers a plain-English completion report to you with what was built and any follow-ups.

workflows.name: Quick Fix
workflows.tagline: A lean path for small, well-understood changes.
workflows.when: Use this for small bugs or tweaks where planning would only slow things down.

steps.title: Report the Bug
steps.act: You flag a small, well-understood bug or change for the guild to fix.

steps.title: Clarify the Ask
steps.act: The Butler sharpens the request into a single, unambiguous fix ticket with clear acceptance.

steps.label: You confirm the fix scope is correct before the Developer starts work.

steps.title: Make the Fix
steps.act: The Developer applies the smallest correct change and tests it locally before handing it off.

steps.title: Verify the Fix
steps.act: The Quartermaster double-checks the change and confirms nothing else broke in the process.

steps.label: The Butler reports the fix and its verification back to you.

workflows.name: Design Sprint
workflows.tagline: From visual concept to shipped UI in one tight sprint.
workflows.when: Use this when the work centers on UI, UX, brand, or visual quality.

steps.title: Request the Sprint
steps.act: You describe the UI or UX problem you want the guild to design around.

steps.title: Shape the Brief
steps.act: The Butler reframes your ask into a focused design brief with clear visual success criteria.

steps.label: You approve the design brief before the sprint officially kicks off.

steps.title: Plan the Sprint
steps.act: The Strategist sequences design, build, and verification into a single tight sprint plan.

steps.title: Design the Visuals
steps.act: The Designer produces the UI/UX mockups, components, and brand treatments for the sprint.

steps.title: Build the Design
steps.act: The Developer implements the design faithfully in code, respecting the visual spec.

steps.title: Clean Up and Verify
steps.act: The Quartermaster polishes the build and verifies the design holds up in real interactions.

steps.label: The Butler summarizes the design sprint outcome, with screenshots and notes, for you.

workflows.name: Architecture Build
workflows.tagline: Schemas, systems, and structural change, done right.
workflows.when: Use this when the work touches the database, system design, or core architecture.

steps.title: Request the Build
steps.act: You describe the system, schema, or architectural change you need the guild to make.

steps.title: Clarify the Ask
steps.act: The Butler rewrites the ask into a precise architecture brief with constraints called out.

steps.label: You approve the architecture brief before scoping and design begin.

steps.title: Scope the Work
steps.act: The Strategist defines the boundaries, milestones, dependencies, and risks of the effort.

steps.title: Design the System
steps.act: The Architect drafts the system design and database schema to satisfy the scope.

steps.label: The Quartermaster certifies the design is sound, consistent, and safe to implement.

steps.title: Build Migrations and Code
steps.act: The Developer writes the schema migrations and implements the system against the certified design.

steps.title: Clean Up and Verify
steps.act: The Quartermaster runs final checks and confirms the architecture works as designed in production-like conditions.

steps.label: The Butler reports the architecture build, its verification, and any caveats to you.

workflows.name: Legal Codex
workflows.tagline: Turn real laws into a clean, multilingual codex.
workflows.when: Use this when you need real laws loaded into a codex and translated across locales.

steps.title: Submit the Law
steps.act: You point the guild at the real-world law or regulation you need codified.

steps.title: Frame the Request
steps.act: The Butler restates the request as a clear codex-loading task with target locales listed.

steps.label: You confirm the scope of the codex work and the target locales before translation begins.

steps.title: Load and Translate
steps.act: The Translator ingests the real law and translates it faithfully across each required locale.

steps.title: Run Publish Check
steps.act: The Quartermaster runs the pre-publish check on the codex and signs off on its readiness.

steps.label: The Butler delivers the codex summary, locales covered, and publication status to you.

workflows.name: Market Recon
workflows.tagline: Read-only research, risk, and market intelligence.
workflows.when: Use this for investment, trading, or market research where no code should be written.

steps.title: Ask the Question
steps.act: You pose a research or investment question you want the guild to analyze.

steps.title: Sharpen the Question
steps.act: The Butler sharpens the question into a focused read-only research brief with clear scope.

steps.label: You approve the research brief and confirm the read-only boundary before analysis begins.

steps.title: Analyze Market and Risk
steps.act: The Merchant investigates the market, prices, and risk in a read-only analysis pass and writes a report.

steps.label: The Butler delivers the Merchant's market and risk report, with caveats, to you.

workflows.name: Skill Forge
workflows.tagline: Keep the guild's skills sharp, synced, and current.
workflows.when: Use this for guild self-maintenance, skill upgrades, or tooling upkeep.

steps.title: Request Skill Maintenance
steps.act: You ask the guild to sync, upgrade, or create skills for the team.

steps.title: Route the Request
steps.act: The Butler classifies the request and routes it to the right maintenance owner.

steps.title: Sync Upgrade Skills
steps.act: The Quartermaster syncs, upgrades, or authors the skills that were requested.

steps.title: Provide Tooling Support
steps.act: The Engineer provides any tooling or dev-environment support the skill changes require.

steps.label: The Butler reports the skill-forge outcome, version changes, and tooling status to you.

workflows.name: Guild Log Sync
workflows.tagline: Sweep recent sessions and backfill any missing guild-log entries.
workflows.when: Use this to reconcile the guild log after a stretch of work — loop the recent sessions, find changes that never got recorded, and log them.

steps.title: Call the Log Sweep
steps.act: You ask the guild to loop the recent sessions (today and yesterday) and check the guild log for any change that never got recorded.

steps.title: Restate the Order
steps.act: Before any work begins, the Butler echoes your request back in plain English — loop today and yesterday's sessions, read the guild log, and log whatever is missing — so you both agree on the quest before he moves.

steps.label: You confirm the Butler understood the order correctly before the sweep begins.

steps.title: Scout the Sessions
steps.act: The Butler, drawing Sonnet, loops every session from today and yesterday, reads the guild log, runs build_guild_log.py --dry-run to surface git-visible gaps, and inspects non-git artifact work (art PNGs, untracked tooling) to assemble a brief of what is missing.

steps.label: The Quartermaster certifies each candidate entry is real, non-duplicate, and correctly typed before anything is written to the log.

steps.title: Log the Missing Entries
steps.act: The Quartermaster, drawing MiniMax M3, runs the two-tier pipeline — build_guild_log.py to backfill git-visible commits, log_event.py for the non-git events — then build.py to regenerate the dashboard data.

steps.label: The Butler reports what was logged — git-tier catch-up plus manual entries — and the new log total, to you.
